maml:description.maml:para: Clear the in-memory cache in UiPathOrch drives.

The UiPathOrch module caches entities retrieved from the Orchestrator on each drive to optimize response times and reduce the load on the Orchestrator server. In particular, this cache is used to display appropriate candidates for auto-completion of parameter values.

If you want the PowerShell console to reflect updates to entities on Orchestrator (such as creating or removing folders) made via Orchestrator Web or other external applications, clear the in-memory cache with this cmdlet.

For the -Path parameter, specify the drive on which you want to clear the cache. If the -Path parameter is not specified, the cache on the current drive is cleared. If the current drive is not an UiPathOrch drive, the cache on all UiPathOrch drives are cleared.
Primary Endpoint: (none)
OAuth required scopes: (none)
Required permissions: (none)
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill AllDrives Description }}
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill AllDrives Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Clear-OrchCache

dev:remarks.maml:para: Clears the in-memory cache on the Orch1: as the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Clear-OrchCache

dev:remarks.maml:para: Clears the in-memory cache on all drives managed by UiPathOrch, because the current drive (C:) is not an UiPathOrch drive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Clear-OrchCache Orch1:,Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Clears the in-memory cache on the Orch1: and Orch2: drives.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Clear-OrchCache .,Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: The current drive can be specified using a period (.), which represents the current location. In this command, the cache is cleared on both the current drive (Orch1:) and Orch2:.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets classifiers of Document Understanding.

Retrieve all classifiers from projects. This cmdlet operates on the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchDu provider. If the scope in the configuration file includes "Du.", the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchDu provider will be automatically added. You can confirm this with the Get-PSDrive cmdlet. The configuration file can be opened with the Edit-OrchConfig cmdlet.
Primary Endpoint: GET /du_/api/framework/projects/{projectId}/classifiers?api-version=1
OAuth Required scopes: Du.Digitization.Api or Du.Classification.Api or Du.Extraction.Api or Du.Validation.Api
Specifies the name of the classifiers to be retrieved.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the alerts.

Alert is a tenant entity. Therefore, specify the drive names as the targets on this cmdlet. This cmdlet always queries the Orchestrator for the most current status. Therefore, note that the output of this cmdlet is not cached.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Alerts
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: Alerts.View
Specifies the timeframe for filtering alerts based on their creation date. Acceptable values include Hour, Day, Week, and more.
Specifies the severity level of the alerts to be retrieved. Acceptable values are Info, Success, Warn, Error, and Fatal.
Specify the Component of the alerts to be retrieved. Component you can specify includes Folders, Robots, Process, and others.
Specifies that only alerts created after the provided datetime value will be returned.
Specifies that only alerts created before the provided datetime value will be returned.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specify the Component of the alerts to be retrieved. Component you can specify includes Folders, Robots, Process, and others.
Specifies that only alerts created after the provided datetime value will be returned.
Specifies that only alerts created before the provided datetime value will be returned.
Specifies the timeframe for filtering alerts based on their creation date. Acceptable values include Hour, Day, Week, and more.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the severity level of the alerts to be retrieved. Acceptable values are Info, Success, Warn, Error, and Fatal.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchAlert

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all alerts on Orch1: drive, which is the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchAlert -Path Orch1:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all alerts on Orch1: drive. This command can be executed on any drive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchAlert -Last Day

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays alerts from the past 24 hours. Additionally, you can specify multiple parameters simultaneously to narrow down the alerts you retrieve. For example, parameters such as `-CreationTimeAfter`, `-Component`, and `-Severity` are available.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchAlert -Skip 3 -First 5

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command retrieves the first 5 alerts after skipping the initial 3 alerts. It is useful for paging through results when you have a large number of alerts and only need to see a specific subset.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the API triggers.

Outputs information about API triggers with specified names in the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no API trigger names are specified, it outputs all API triggers in the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/HttpTriggers
OAuth required scopes: (undocumented)
Required permissions:
Specifies the Name of the API triggers to be retrieved.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the Name of the API triggers to be retrieved.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchApiTrigger

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all API triggers in the 'Shared' folder, which is the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchApiTrigger -Recurse

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all API triggers in the current folder and all its subfolders. When run in the root folder, it shows all API triggers across all folders in that tenant.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchApiTrigger -Recurse <API trigger names>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays API triggers with the names specified in the current folder and its subfolders. This is useful for identifying which folders contain API triggers with a specified names. You can specify multiple API trigger names, separated by commas, including wildcards. API trigger names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchApiTrigger -Path <folder names> <API trigger names>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays API triggers with the names specified in the specified folders. You can specify multiple folders, separated by commas, including wildcards.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchApiTrigger -Recurse -Path Orch1:\,Orch2:\

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all API triggers in Orch1: and Orch2:.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchApiTrigger -Recurse | select Path,Id,Name

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays output with only the selected columns. Specify multiple columns separated by commas, including wildcards. Column names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchApiTrigger -Recurse | Export-Csv c:apiTriggers.csv

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports the output to a CSV file. The CSV file will be located at the current location of the C: drive. You can customize the CSV format by combining with `select` to specify which columns to include. Try `ii c:' to open the current location of the C: drive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 8 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchApiTrigger -Recurse | ConvertTo-Json

dev:remarks.maml:para: Converts the output to JSON format, providing a raw view of the data from Orchestrator.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the execution settings configuration.

Gets the execution settings configuration (display name, value type, etc.). If scope is Global, the default values will be the initial ones. If scope is Robot, then the default values will be the actual values set globally. e.g., Resolution width Assume it was set globally to 720. Then within the config returned by this function, the default value for this setting will be 0 if the scope is Global and 720 if the scope is Robot.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetExecutionSettingsConfiguration(scope={scope})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read

maml:description.maml:para: Get the machines assigned to folders.

Outputs information about machines with specified names that are assigned to the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no machine names are specified, it outputs all machines assigned to the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachinesForFolder(key={folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: (Units.View or SubFolders.View - Gets machines for any folder or only if user has SubFolders.View permission on folder)
Specifies the Name of the folder machines to be retrieved.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
{{ Fill CsvEncoding Description }}
{{ Fill ExportCsv Description }}
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the Name of the folder machines to be retrieved.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
{{ Fill CsvEncoding Description }}
{{ Fill ExportCsv Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchFolderMachine

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all machines assigned to the 'Shared' folder, which is the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Recurse

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all machines assigned to the current folder and all its subfolders. When run in the root folder, it shows all machines assigned across all folders in that tenant.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Recurse <machine names>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays machines with the names specified assigned to the current folder and its subfolders. This is useful for identifying which folders contain machines with a specified names. You can specify multiple machine names, separated by commas, including wildcards. Machine names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Path <folder names> <machine names>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays machines with the names specified assigned to the specified folders. You can specify multiple folders, separated by commas, including wildcards.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Recurse -Path Orch1:\,Orch2:\

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all the machines assigned to Orch1: and Orch2:.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Recurse | select Path,Id,Name

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays output with only the selected columns. Specify multiple columns separated by commas, including wildcards. Column names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Recurse | Export-Csv c:folderMachines.csv

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports the output to a CSV file. The CSV file will be located at the current location of the C: drive. You can customize the CSV format by combining with `select` to specify which columns to include. Try `ii c:' to open the current location of the C: drive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 8 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Recurse | ConvertTo-Json

dev:remarks.maml:para: Converts the output to JSON format, providing a raw view of the data from Orchestrator.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId

dev:remarks.maml:para: Outputs the issuance date and time of client secrets for all machines in this tenant.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId <machine names>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Outputs the issuance date and time of client secrets for the specified machines.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId | ? CreationTime -LT '2024/10/01' | Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret

dev:remarks.maml:para: Deletes all client secrets issued before 2024/10/01 for all machines in this tenant.

maml:description.maml:para: Get the triggers.

Outputs information about triggers with specified names in the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no trigger names are specified, it outputs all triggers in the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/ProcessSchedules, GET /odata/ProcessSchedules({processScheduleId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs or OR.Jobs.Read
Required permissions: Schedules.View
Specifies the Name of the triggers to be retrieved.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
{{ Fill CsvEncoding Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchTrigger

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all triggers in the 'Shared' folder, which is the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchTrigger -Recurse

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all triggers in the current folder and all its subfolders. When run in the root folder, it shows all triggers across all folders in that tenant.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchTrigger -Recurse <trigger names>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays triggers with the names specified in the current folder and its subfolders. This is useful for identifying which folders contain triggers with a specified names. You can specify multiple trigger names, separated by commas, including wildcards. Trigger names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchTrigger -Path <folder names> <trigger names>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays triggers with the names specified in the specified folders. You can specify multiple folders, separated by commas, including wildcards.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTrigger -Recurse -Path Orch1:\,Orch2:\

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all triggers in Orch1: and Orch2:.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTrigger -Recurse | select Path,Id,Name

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays output with only the selected columns. Specify multiple columns separated by commas, including wildcards. Column names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTrigger -Recurse | Export-Csv c:triggers.csv

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports the output to a CSV file. The CSV file will be located at the current location of the C: drive. You can customize the CSV format by combining with `select` to specify which columns to include. Try `ii c:' to open the current location of the C: drive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 8 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTrigger -Recurse | ConvertTo-Json

dev:remarks.maml:para: Converts the output to JSON format, providing a raw view of the data from Orchestrator.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the assets.

Removes assets with specified names from the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Assets({assetId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Assets
Required permissions: Assets.Delete
Specifies the Name of the assets to be removed.
Specifies the ValueType of the assets to be removed.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the requirements from projects in Test Manager.

Removes the requirements from projects. This cmdlet operates on the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider. If the scope in the configuration file includes "TM.", the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider will be automatically added. You can confirm this with the Get-PSDrive cmdlet. maml:description.maml:para: Sets the current location to the UiPathOrch module's installation directory.

The Set-OrchLocation cmdlet changes the current location (working directory) to the directory where the UiPathOrch module is installed. This allows for quick navigation to the module's files and resources.

OAuth required scopes: (none)
Required permissions: (none)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-OrchLocation

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command sets the current location to the installation directory of the UiPathOrch module.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-OrchLocation UiPath.PowerShell.OrchProvider

dev:remarks.maml:para: You can specify the module name explicitly.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates or updates robot accounts in the Identity server.

Updates the groups to which the robot account in the Identity server belongs. If a non-existent robot account name is specified, a new robot account will be created with that name and added to the specified group.

Multiple robot account names can be specified, separated by commas. However, please note that wildcards cannot be used in the account name. This is a trade-off for this cmdlet's ability to both create and update. For the group names, you can specify multiple comma-separated texts containing wildcards.

You can also create or update a robot account by importing a CSV file. To generate a CSV file in an importable format, run `Get-OrchPmRobot -ExportCsv`. To see how to import this CSV file, refer to Example 2.
The column names in the CSV file correspond to each parameter name. Please refer to the help for each parameter.
Primary Endpoint:
OAuth required scopes:
Required permissions:

Specifies the name of the group(s) to which the specified robot account belongs. The specified robot account will be removed from any group that is not specified here. To specify GroupName in a CSV file, name the columns GroupName0, GroupName1, ..., with one group name in each. You can use up to GroupName9.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies the name of the group(s) to which the specified robot account belongs. The specified robot account will be removed from any group that is not specified here. To specify GroupName in a CSV file, name the columns GroupName0, GroupName1, ..., with one group name in each. You can use up to GroupName9.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Set-OrchPmRobotAccount RobotAccountName MyGroup,YourGroup

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the groups for the robot account `RobotAccountName` to `MyGroup` and `YourGroup`. If a robot account with the name `RobotAccountName` does not exist, a new robot account will be created with this name and added to `MyGroup` and `YourGroup`.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Import-Csv c:ExportedPmRobotAccount.csv | Set-OrchPmRobotAccount

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports a CSV file to create or update robot accounts in the Identity server. To generate a CSV file in an importable format, run `Get-OrchPmRobot -ExportCsv`. Please note that the Path column specified in the CSV file takes precedence over the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Import-Csv c:ExportedPmRobotAccount.csv | Set-OrchPmRobotAccount -Path .

dev:remarks.maml:para: Any column in the CSV file can be overridden by specifying the parameter on the command line in the PS console. For example, to add a robot account to the tenant in the current drive instead of the drive specified in the Path column of the CSV file, specify `-Path .`. Alternatively, you can simply import a CSV file with the Path column removed to add/update robot accounts in the tenant of the current drive.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds or updates a role.

You can import a CSV file exported with Get-OrchRole -ExportCsv. When importing to a different tenant than the one from which the file was exported, please make sure to appropriately modify the Path column in the CSV file. Alternatively, you can specify the destination drive name with the -Path parameter when importing with Set-OrchRole to overwrite the Path values in the CSV file.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Roles&$expand=Permissions, POST /odata/Roles, PUT /odata/Roles({role.Id}
